How to Enable 2FA on Discord (Step-by-Step Guide to Protect Your Account)

Discord supports 2FA (Two-factor Authentication) out of the box, and enabling it is a really good idea — it stops your account from getting hijacked and spamming weird stuff to your friends. So here's a quick walk through how to turn it on.
2FA stands for Two-factor Authentication. It's also called dual-factor auth or two-step verification.
The main benefit of 2FA is preventing your account from getting taken over and used to send sketchy messages. Common Discord scams include things like:
These messages usually come with a link asking you to click. Quick reminder: there's no free lunch. Nobody's going to randomly send you free Nitro or Classic.
These scams exist to get you to click the link so the attacker can grab your account details — phone number, email, etc.
To keep your account safe (and your friends safe from your account spamming them), turning on 2FA is the way to go.
You should land on the "My Account" page. Scroll down and you'll see a "Password and Authentication" section. Right below that is "Two-Factor Authentication" — click "
It'll ask for your Discord password to confirm:

Now download Google Authenticator on your phone and scan the QR code (or enter the 2FA key manually). The app will start showing a 6-digit code that refreshes regularly. Type that code into the box at the bottom of Discord:
(Heads up: the codes are time-limited, so type fast.)
Once 2FA is active, Discord will suggest setting up "SMS authentication" and "Download backup codes". Both are worth doing in case something happens to your phone:
Don't post your backup codes anywhere online — anyone with one can log in.
If you lose them, you can regenerate or view them again. Each backup code can only be used once, so the UI also shows which ones have already been used:
Viewing backup codes again will ask for your Discord password:

How do you enable 2FA (two-factor authentication) on Discord? This step-by-step guide walks through setting it up with Google Authenticator, plus SMS authorization and backup codes so you won't get locked out if your phone dies.
